tasks.title: Initialize Next.js Project with Stripe Apps SDK
tasks.description: Scaffold the project with Next.js 15+ (App Router), integrate Stripe Apps SDK, and configure the development environment for building inside the Stripe Dashboard.
tasks.details:
1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest winback --typescript --app --tailwind`
2. Install Stripe Apps SDK: `npm install @stripe/ui-extension-sdk @stripe/stripe-js stripe`
3. Configure stripe-app.json manifest with required permissions: read_disputes, read_charges, read_customers, write_dispute_evidence
4. Set up the app entry point per Stripe Apps docs
5. Configure environment variables for STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_APP_SECRET, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
6. Verify app loads in Stripe Developer Dashboard dev mode
tasks.testStrategy: App loads successfully in Stripe Developer Dashboard without errors. All environment variables validated. TypeScript compiles without errors.

tasks.title: Set Up Supabase Project with Database Schema
tasks.description: Create Supabase project and deploy the database schema for merchants, playbooks, disputes, and evidence files. Configure Row Level Security policies.
tasks.details:
1. Create Supabase project
2. Run migration with tables: merchants, playbooks, disputes, evidence_files (per PRD schema)
3. Enable RLS on all tables
4. Create RLS policies: merchants can only access their own data
5. Set up Supabase Storage bucket 'evidence' with 10MB file size limit
6. Configure 30-day auto-deletion policy on evidence files
7. Install Supabase client in Next.js: `npm install @supabase/supabase-js`
8. Set up SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY env vars
tasks.testStrategy: All tables created with correct schema. RLS policies prevent cross-merchant data access. Storage bucket accepts file uploads up to 10MB. Auto-deletion policy configured.

tasks.title: Research Stripe App Marketplace Billing Model
tasks.description: Research whether to use Stripe's built-in app pricing (marketplace billing with revenue share) or self-managed Stripe Billing. Document findings and make recommendation.
tasks.details:
1. Review Stripe App Marketplace developer docs for billing options
2. Check if marketplace supports $29/month flat-rate subscriptions
3. Understand Stripe's revenue share percentage for marketplace billing
4. Compare: marketplace billing (simpler, Stripe takes cut) vs self-managed Stripe Billing (full control, no cut)
5. Check if free trial is supported in marketplace billing
6. Document findings in .taskmaster/docs/billing-research.md
7. Make recommendation based on: simplicity, margin impact, merchant experience
tasks.testStrategy: Research document created with clear recommendation, revenue share percentages, and implementation approach.

tasks.title: Research Stripe Apps SDK UI Constraints
tasks.description: Investigate Stripe Apps SDK UI component library, viewport limitations, custom UI capabilities, and network access restrictions to confirm the planned UX is achievable.
tasks.details:
1. Review Stripe Apps SDK UI component library documentation
2. List all available components (Box, Button, TextField, Select, etc.)
3. Test viewport dimensions and scrolling behavior
4. Confirm file upload capability within iframe constraints
5. Test network access from frontend (can it call external APIs? Likely not — confirm backend needed)
6. Check if rich text editing is possible for narrative review
7. Document constraints and any workarounds needed
8. Output: .taskmaster/docs/sdk-constraints.md
tasks.testStrategy: Constraints document created. Each planned UX feature mapped to available SDK capabilities. Blockers identified with workarounds.

tasks.title: Research Stripe Disputes API Evidence Submission
tasks.description: Research how the Stripe Disputes API accepts evidence files and narrative text. Document the evidence field mapping per reason code.
tasks.details:
1. Review Stripe Disputes API documentation for evidence submission
2. Test POST /v1/disputes/{id}/evidence with test mode disputes
3. Document which evidence fields exist (shipping_documentation, customer_communication, receipt, service_documentation, etc.)
4. Map each MVP reason code to the appropriate Stripe evidence fields
5. Determine file format and size requirements
6. Check if files are uploaded directly or via URLs
7. Output: .taskmaster/docs/disputes-api-research.md
tasks.testStrategy: Can successfully submit test evidence to a Stripe test mode dispute. All evidence fields documented with reason code mappings.

tasks.title: Design and Deploy Landing Page with Waitlist
tasks.description: Build and deploy a marketing landing page with email waitlist capture. Deploy early to start building awareness during the app build phase.
tasks.details:
1. Create a standalone Next.js page or use a subdirectory route
2. Design with clear value prop: '$29/month flat, no success fees'
3. Include competitive comparison section (vs Smart Disputes 30%, Chargeflow 25%, DisputeNinja $499)
4. Add email waitlist form (store in Supabase 'waitlist' table or use a form provider)
5. Mobile responsive design
6. SEO optimization: target 'how to win Stripe dispute', 'Stripe chargeback help'
7. Deploy to Vercel under project domain
8. Set up meta tags, OG images
tasks.testStrategy: Landing page loads on all viewports. Email capture stores submissions. SEO meta tags present. Page scores well on Lighthouse.

tasks.title: Research Visa Core Rules for MVP Reason Codes
tasks.description: Ingest Visa Core Rules documentation to draft playbook content for reason codes 13.1 (Not Received), 13.2 (Cancelled Recurring), 13.3 (Not as Described), and 10.4 (Fraud - Card Absent).
tasks.details:
1. Find and review Visa Core Rules & Visa Product and Service Rules (publicly available portions)
2. For each reason code (13.1, 13.2, 13.3, 10.4), extract:
   - Official definition and cardholder claim
   - Issuer evaluation criteria (what the bank looks at)
   - Required compelling evidence (mandatory vs helpful)
   - Time limits and procedural requirements
   - Specific conditions under which merchant wins/loses
3. Research merchant community forums for real-world win/loss patterns
4. Draft playbook content per PRD playbook structure
5. Output: .taskmaster/docs/playbooks/visa-13.1.md, visa-13.2.md, visa-13.3.md, visa-10.4.md
tasks.testStrategy: Each playbook draft contains: plain English explanation, issuer evaluation criteria, evidence checklist (mandatory/helpful), common mistakes, narrative template structure. Joe validates for domain accuracy.

tasks.title: Research Mastercard Chargeback Guide for Reason Code 4853
tasks.description: Ingest Mastercard Chargeback Guide documentation to draft playbook content for reason code 4853 (Cardholder Dispute - Not as Described).
tasks.details:
1. Find and review Mastercard Chargeback Guide (publicly available portions)
2. For reason code 4853, extract:
   - Official definition and cardholder claim
   - Issuer evaluation criteria
   - Required compelling evidence
   - Time limits and procedural requirements
   - Win/loss conditions
3. Research merchant community forums for real-world patterns
4. Draft playbook content per PRD structure
5. Output: .taskmaster/docs/playbooks/mastercard-4853.md
tasks.testStrategy: Playbook draft contains all required sections. Joe validates for domain accuracy.

tasks.title: Build Stripe App Shell and Sidebar Navigation
tasks.description: Build the main Stripe App container with sidebar navigation, routing between views (disputes list, dispute detail, settings), and app branding.
tasks.details:
1. Set up Stripe App entry point with proper manifest
2. Create main app layout with sidebar navigation using Stripe UI components
3. Set up routing between views: Disputes List, Dispute Detail, Settings/Account
4. Add WinBack branding (logo, colors) within SDK constraints
5. Implement loading states and error boundaries
6. Set up app-to-backend communication layer (authenticated API calls to Vercel)
tasks.testStrategy: App loads in Stripe Dashboard dev mode. Navigation between views works. Backend API calls authenticated correctly.

tasks.title: Implement Stripe OAuth and API Client
tasks.description: Set up Stripe OAuth authentication for the app and create API client utilities for reading disputes, charges, and customers from the merchant's Stripe account.
tasks.details:
1. Implement Stripe App OAuth flow per Stripe Apps authentication docs
2. Store merchant's Stripe credentials securely (Supabase merchants table)
3. Create Stripe API client wrapper with methods:
   - listDisputes(accountId) - list all disputes
   - getDispute(accountId, disputeId) - get dispute details
   - getCharge(accountId, chargeId) - get charge details
   - getCustomer(accountId, customerId) - get customer info
4. Handle API errors, rate limiting, and retries
5. Create merchant record in Supabase on first auth
tasks.testStrategy: OAuth flow completes successfully. API client retrieves test disputes, charges, and customers. Rate limiting handled gracefully.

tasks.title: Build Dispute List View with Status Indicators
tasks.description: Build the main disputes dashboard showing all active disputes with status indicators, amounts, reason codes, and deadline urgency.
tasks.details:
1. Create disputes list component using Stripe UI components (Table/List)
2. Display for each dispute: amount, reason code, customer name, status, response deadline
3. Color-coded urgency indicators: green (14+ days), yellow (5-13), red (<5)
4. Status badges: needs_response, evidence_submitted, won, lost
5. Sort by urgency (closest deadline first) by default
6. Filter/sort controls for status and date
7. Handle empty state (no disputes - show onboarding message)
8. Pull dispute data from Stripe API via backend
tasks.testStrategy: Disputes list renders with correct data from Stripe test mode. Urgency colors display correctly. Sorting and filtering work. Empty state shows onboarding content.

tasks.title: Build Dispute Detail View with Stripe Data Auto-Population
tasks.description: Build the dispute detail view that shows full dispute information, auto-populated from Stripe data, with the reason code context and game plan.
tasks.details:
1. Create dispute detail layout with sections: Overview, Evidence Checklist, Upload, Narrative, Submit
2. Auto-populate from Stripe: dispute amount, reason code, customer name, transaction date, charge details, payment method info
3. Display response deadline with countdown timer
4. Show plain English explanation of the reason code (from playbook)
5. Display 'game plan' summary: what the issuer looks at, how to win this type
6. Smart urgency: if <5 days, show streamlined essentials-only view
tasks.testStrategy: Detail view renders with correct Stripe data. Countdown timer updates. Plain English explanation matches the reason code. Urgency mode triggers correctly at <5 days.

tasks.title: Seed Playbook Data into Supabase
tasks.description: Create playbook data model in Supabase and seed the 5 validated MVP playbooks (Visa 13.1, 13.2, 13.3, 10.4, Mastercard 4853).
tasks.details:
1. Finalize playbook JSON structure matching the Supabase schema:
   - network, reason_code, display_name, description
   - issuer_evaluation, evidence_checklist (JSONB array), common_mistakes
   - narrative_template, pro_tips, urgency_essentials
2. Convert validated playbook markdown into structured JSON
3. Create seed script or migration to insert 5 playbooks
4. Verify all playbooks load correctly via Supabase client
5. Create API route: GET /api/playbooks/:network/:reasonCode
tasks.testStrategy: All 5 playbooks exist in Supabase. API route returns correct playbook for each reason code. Evidence checklists render with mandatory/helpful flags.

tasks.title: Build Evidence Checklist Component
tasks.description: Build the interactive evidence checklist component that shows reason-code-specific items with 'why this matters' expandable explanations and completion tracking.
tasks.details:
1. Create checklist component that renders items from playbook evidence_checklist
2. Each item shows: checkbox, item name, required/helpful badge
3. Expandable 'Why this matters' section per item with issuer evaluation context
4. Items that Stripe auto-fills (transaction data) marked as auto-populated
5. Smart urgency: when <5 days, show only urgency_essentials subset
6. Track completion state per dispute in Supabase
7. Visual progress indicator showing X of Y items completed
tasks.testStrategy: Checklist renders correctly for each of the 5 reason codes. 'Why this matters' expands/collapses. Urgency mode shows reduced checklist. Progress tracking persists.

tasks.title: Build Smart Urgency Deadline Display
tasks.description: Implement the adaptive urgency UX that changes the workflow based on time remaining before the dispute response deadline.
tasks.details:
1. Calculate days remaining from Stripe dispute response_by field
2. Build countdown timer component (days, hours remaining)
3. Color coding: green (14+), yellow (5-13), red (<5)
4. At <5 days: switch to 'essentials only' mode
   - Reduce checklist to urgency_essentials from playbook
   - Show banner: 'Deadline is close. Focus on critical evidence.'
5. Never block submission regardless of completeness
6. Timer visible on every screen during dispute response flow
tasks.testStrategy: Timer displays correct countdown. Colors change at correct thresholds. Essentials-only mode triggers at <5 days with reduced checklist. Submission never blocked.

tasks.title: Build Evidence Upload Component
tasks.description: Build per-checklist-item file upload component that stores evidence files temporarily in Supabase Storage.
tasks.details:
1. Create upload component attached to each checklist item
2. Support drag-and-drop and file picker
3. Accept: PDF, PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, CSV, TXT (up to 10MB per file)
4. Upload to Supabase Storage bucket 'evidence' with path: {merchant_id}/{dispute_id}/{checklist_item_key}/{filename}
5. Create evidence_files record in Supabase linking file to dispute and checklist item
6. Show upload progress, success/failure states
7. Allow replace and remove of uploaded files
8. Display file preview/thumbnail where possible
tasks.testStrategy: Files upload successfully for each type. 10MB limit enforced. Files link to correct dispute and checklist item. Replace and remove work. Supabase Storage stores files correctly.

tasks.title: Design AI Prompt Templates Per Reason Code
tasks.description: Design Claude API prompt templates for each of the 5 MVP reason codes, structured to generate compelling narratives with reasoning annotations from uploaded evidence.
tasks.details:
1. Create prompt template structure:
   - System prompt: 'You are a payments dispute expert...'
   - Include reason code playbook context (issuer evaluation criteria, required evidence)
   - Inject: Stripe transaction data, uploaded evidence metadata, checklist completion status
   - Output format: structured narrative with reasoning annotations like '[ADDRESSES: Visa 13.1 delivery proof requirement]'
2. Create 5 prompt templates, one per reason code
3. Test each template with sample data to verify quality
4. Include instruction to only reference evidence that was actually uploaded (no fabrication)
5. Store templates in playbook.narrative_template field or separate config
tasks.testStrategy: Each prompt template generates a coherent, evidence-based narrative. Reasoning annotations present and accurate. No hallucinated evidence. Output is professional and concise.

tasks.title: Build Claude API Integration Backend Route
tasks.description: Create the Vercel API route that calls Claude API to generate dispute response narratives from the prompt templates and uploaded evidence.
tasks.details:
1. Create API route: POST /api/narratives/generate
2. Input: dispute_id, reason_code, evidence_metadata, stripe_transaction_data
3. Load prompt template for the reason code
4. Inject evidence metadata and Stripe data into prompt
5. Call Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-6 for cost efficiency)
6. Parse response, extract narrative and reasoning annotations
7. Return structured response with narrative text and annotations
8. Handle Claude API errors gracefully (timeout, rate limit, downtime)
9. Track generation count per dispute (increment narrative_generations_count)
10. Enforce 5-generation limit per dispute
tasks.testStrategy: API route generates narrative from test data. Response includes reasoning annotations. Generation count increments. 5-generation limit enforced. Error handling works for API failures.

tasks.title: Build Narrative Review/Edit UI with Reasoning Annotations
tasks.description: Build the narrative review interface where merchants can read the AI-generated narrative, see reasoning annotations, and edit before submission.
tasks.details:
1. Display AI-generated narrative in a reviewable format
2. Show reasoning annotations inline: 'This section addresses...' with expandable explanations
3. Make narrative editable (text area or rich text editor within SDK constraints)
4. Show generation count: 'Generation 2 of 5'
5. 'Regenerate' button to request a new narrative (with confirmation if edits will be lost)
6. 'Approve & Continue to Submit' button
7. Handle loading state during generation (<15 second target)
8. Fallback if Claude API is down: allow manual narrative entry
tasks.testStrategy: Narrative displays with reasoning annotations. Edit functionality works. Regenerate creates a new version. Generation counter accurate. Manual fallback works when API unavailable.

tasks.title: Build Stripe Disputes API Evidence Submission Flow
tasks.description: Implement the one-click evidence submission that packages all uploaded files and narrative, maps to Stripe's evidence fields, and submits via the Disputes API.
tasks.details:
1. Create API route: POST /api/disputes/{dispute_id}/submit
2. Gather: all uploaded evidence files, generated narrative, Stripe auto-populated data
3. Map evidence to Stripe's expected fields per reason code (per research from Task 5)
4. Submit via Stripe Disputes API: POST /v1/disputes/{dispute_id}/evidence
5. Handle file uploads to Stripe (base64 or URL, per API requirements)
6. Update dispute status to 'evidence_submitted' in Supabase
7. Record submission timestamp
8. Build confirmation screen: 'Evidence submitted. Typical resolution: 60-75 days for Visa.'
9. Handle submission errors with clear messaging and retry option
10. Never block submission even if evidence is incomplete (warn, don't block)
tasks.testStrategy: Evidence submits successfully to Stripe test mode dispute. All files and narrative attached. Status updates in Supabase. Confirmation screen displays. Error handling works.

tasks.title: Implement Webhook Endpoint for Dispute Outcome Updates
tasks.description: Create webhook endpoint to receive Stripe dispute status updates (won, lost, expired) and update tracking in Supabase.
tasks.details:
1. Create API route: POST /api/webhooks/stripe
2. Verify Stripe webhook signature
3. Handle events: charge.dispute.closed, charge.dispute.updated, charge.dispute.created
4. On dispute.created: create dispute record in Supabase, trigger notification
5. On dispute.closed: update status to 'won' or 'lost' based on dispute.status
6. On dispute.updated: update dispute data in Supabase
7. Configure webhook in Stripe Dashboard for these events
8. Add retry/idempotency handling
tasks.testStrategy: Webhook receives and validates Stripe test events. Dispute records update correctly. Duplicate events handled idempotently.

tasks.title: Build Win Rate Calculation and Dashboard Display
tasks.description: Calculate and display the merchant's dispute win rate over time, with breakdown by reason code.
tasks.details:
1. Query disputes by merchant where status is 'won' or 'lost'
2. Calculate overall win rate: won / (won + lost) * 100
3. Calculate win rate per reason code
4. Display prominently on disputes dashboard: 'Your win rate: X%'
5. Show breakdown: 'Product Not Received: 3/4 won (75%)'
6. Handle edge cases: no completed disputes yet, all wins, all losses
7. Consider trend display: win rate over last 30/60/90 days
tasks.testStrategy: Win rate calculates correctly with test data. Displays accurately on dashboard. Per-reason-code breakdown renders. Edge cases handled.

tasks.title: Build Basic Pattern Detection
tasks.description: Implement basic pattern detection that identifies recurring dispute types and surfaces prevention suggestions to merchants.
tasks.details:
1. Analyze disputes by reason code frequency per merchant
2. Detect patterns: 'You've had 3 Product Not Received disputes this month'
3. Surface prevention suggestions based on patterns:
   - Frequent 13.1 (Not Received): 'Consider requiring signature confirmation for orders over $100'
   - Frequent 13.2 (Cancelled Recurring): 'Ensure your cancellation policy is clear and easy to find'
4. Display pattern alerts on dashboard
5. Only show when enough data exists (minimum 3 disputes of same type)
tasks.testStrategy: Patterns detected correctly with test data. Prevention tips display for common patterns. No false positives with low data volume.

tasks.title: Implement Subscription Billing
tasks.description: Implement $29/month subscription billing based on the billing research findings (marketplace billing or self-managed Stripe Billing).
tasks.details:
1. Based on Task 3 research, implement chosen billing approach:
   Option A (Marketplace): Configure Stripe App pricing in stripe-app.json
   Option B (Self-managed): Create Stripe Price ($29/month recurring), implement checkout flow
2. Create subscription status check middleware
3. Build paywall: if no active subscription, show pricing page with subscribe button
4. Handle billing states: trial, active, past_due, cancelled
5. Show billing status in settings view
6. Handle cancellation flow
7. Store subscription_id and subscription_status in merchants table
tasks.testStrategy: Subscription creates successfully. Paywall blocks access without subscription. Billing status displays correctly. Cancellation works. Webhook updates subscription status.

tasks.title: Build Empty State and Onboarding Flow
tasks.description: Build the empty state experience for merchants with no active disputes, including onboarding content and notification setup.
tasks.details:
1. Detect when merchant has zero disputes
2. Display clean empty state: 'You're all set! When a dispute comes in, we'll walk you through exactly what to do.'
3. Brief onboarding flow on first visit:
   - What WinBack does
   - How it works (checklist → evidence → AI narrative → submit)
   - What happens when a dispute arrives
4. Promise of notification: 'We'll alert you the moment a dispute needs attention'
5. Optional: quick tour of the app interface
tasks.testStrategy: Empty state renders when no disputes exist. Onboarding content is clear and helpful. First-visit detection works.

tasks.title: Build New Dispute Notification/Badge
tasks.description: Implement notification system that alerts merchants when a new dispute arrives and needs attention.
tasks.details:
1. On webhook dispute.created event, mark dispute as needing attention
2. Display badge/indicator in Stripe App sidebar: 'New dispute needs attention'
3. Show notification count on app icon if SDK supports it
4. Clear notification when merchant opens the dispute
5. Consider using Stripe's app notification system if available
tasks.testStrategy: Badge appears when new dispute created via webhook. Badge clears when dispute opened. Count is accurate.

tasks.title: End-to-End QA in Stripe Test Mode
tasks.description: Comprehensive QA testing of the full dispute response flow in Stripe test mode: view dispute → checklist → upload evidence → AI narrative → review → submit.
tasks.details:
1. Create test disputes in Stripe test mode for each of the 5 reason codes
2. Test full flow for each:
   a. Dispute appears in list with correct urgency
   b. Detail view shows correct reason code info and checklist
   c. Evidence upload works for all file types
   d. AI narrative generates with reasoning annotations
   e. Edit and regenerate narrative works
   f. One-click submission succeeds
   g. Confirmation screen shows correctly
3. Test urgency modes: 14+ days, 5-13 days, <5 days
4. Test billing: subscribe, paywall, cancellation
5. Test empty state and onboarding
6. Test webhook outcome updates
7. Document any bugs and fix critical issues
tasks.testStrategy: All 5 reason code flows pass end-to-end. No critical bugs. All urgency modes work. Billing flow complete. Evidence submitted to Stripe successfully.

tasks.title: Prepare Stripe App Marketplace Listing
tasks.description: Create the marketplace listing including description, screenshots, pricing info, and support details for the Stripe App Marketplace submission.
tasks.details:
1. Write marketplace listing:
   - Title: 'WinBack: Win More Chargebacks for $29/month'
   - Description: Lead with value prop ('Stop giving Stripe 30% of your recovered disputes')
   - Feature list highlighting guided evidence, AI narratives, flat pricing
2. Take screenshots showing:
   - Dispute list with urgency indicators
   - Evidence checklist with 'why this matters'
   - AI-generated narrative with reasoning
   - Win rate dashboard
3. Set pricing: $29/month
4. Add support contact info
5. Review Stripe App Marketplace submission requirements
tasks.testStrategy: Listing is complete with all required fields. Screenshots are high quality and show key features. Pricing is correct.

tasks.title: Submit App for Stripe Marketplace Review
tasks.description: Submit the WinBack Stripe App for marketplace review. Address any review feedback.
tasks.details:
1. Review Stripe's app review checklist and requirements
2. Ensure app meets all security and data handling requirements
3. Submit app through Stripe Developer Dashboard
4. Track review status
5. Address any feedback from Stripe review team
6. Plan for potential review timeline (days to weeks)
tasks.testStrategy: App submitted successfully. Review feedback addressed promptly. App approved for marketplace listing.

tasks.title: Initialize Git Repository
tasks.description: [REVIEW FINDING] Initialize git repo — prerequisite missing from original breakdown. Cannot version control anything without this.
tasks.details:
1. git init in project root
2. Create .gitignore (Node.js template + .env, .env.local, Supabase secrets)
3. Initial commit with existing files (CLAUDE.md, .taskmaster/, .gitignore)

This must be the very first task executed.
tasks.testStrategy: Git repo initialized. .gitignore covers node_modules, .env*, .next, Supabase secrets. Initial commit created.

tasks.title: Implement Auth Middleware — Stripe App Signature Verification
tasks.description: [REVIEW FINDING — CRITICAL] Without this, the entire Vercel backend is an open API. Stripe Apps SDK provides fetchStripeSignature() to sign requests from the iframe. Backend must verify on every request.
tasks.details:
1. Research Stripe Apps SDK fetchStripeSignature() during Task 4
2. Create shared middleware: lib/middleware/verifyStripeAppSignature.ts
3. Apply to ALL /api/* routes (except /api/webhooks/stripe which uses webhook signature)
4. Return 401 if signature missing or invalid
5. Extract merchant identity from verified signature
6. Unit tests for valid, invalid, and missing signatures
tasks.testStrategy: Middleware verifies Stripe App signature. Unauthenticated requests return 401. Merchant identity extracted. Webhook route uses separate verification.

tasks.title: Set Up Sentry Error Tracking
tasks.description: [REVIEW FINDING] PRD mentions Sentry but no task creates the integration. Observability is scope, not afterthought.
tasks.details:
1. npm install @sentry/nextjs
2. npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i nextjs
3. Configure Sentry DSN in environment variables
4. Set up source maps for Vercel deployment
5. Add structured context: merchant_id, dispute_id, request_id
6. Configure alert rules for critical errors
tasks.testStrategy: Sentry SDK installed. Errors captured with context. Source maps uploaded on deploy. Alert rules configured.

tasks.title: Implement Supabase Storage TTL Cleanup (30-day evidence expiry)
tasks.description: [REVIEW FINDING] Supabase Storage does NOT have native TTL/auto-deletion. PRD assumes 30-day cleanup but nothing implements it.
tasks.details:
1. Create Supabase Edge Function or database function
2. Query evidence_files where expires_at < NOW()
3. Delete files from Storage and records from DB
4. Set up daily cron trigger (pg_cron or Supabase scheduled function)
5. Log deletions for audit trail
tasks.testStrategy: Files older than 30 days automatically deleted. Corresponding records cleaned up. Runs daily. Deletions logged.

tasks.title: Build Prompt Eval Suite for AI Narrative Quality
tasks.description: [REVIEW FINDING] AI output quality cannot be verified manually at scale. Need automated quality checks for each reason code prompt template.
tasks.details:
1. Create test fixtures: sample dispute data + evidence for 5 reason codes
2. Define quality criteria: no hallucination, annotations present, addresses issuer criteria, professional tone
3. Build eval runner calling Claude API with test fixtures
4. Score against criteria, flag regressions
5. Store baseline outputs
6. Run via npm run eval:prompts
tasks.testStrategy: 5 test fixtures created. Quality scoring automated. Eval suite runnable. Baselines stored. Hallucination check validates evidence references.

tasks.title: Add Narrative Feedback Mechanism on Regenerate
tasks.description: [REVIEW FINDING — Outside Voice] Blind regeneration is wasteful. Capture WHY the merchant rejected the narrative to improve the next generation.
tasks.details:
1. On Regenerate click, show: 'What would you like to change?'
2. Quick options: Too formal, Missing evidence, Inaccurate, Too long, Other
3. Optional free-text field
4. Feed feedback into regeneration prompt
5. Store feedback in Supabase for prompt improvement
6. Skip option available for quick regen
tasks.testStrategy: Feedback prompt shown on regenerate. Feedback injected into prompt. Stored in Supabase. Skip option works.
